description: Fitz, Simmons, and an alien are forced into a deadly game where they must physically restrain a deadly blade, with their lives on the line. As gamblers place bets, Izel suddenly intervenes, offering to buy FitzSimmons, and successfully bribes Kitson. The deadly game ends with Izel saving their lives and introducing her mysterious agenda.
location: Kitson (alien casino planet / House of Games)

transcript.line: Are you ready for a deadly game... from the early days of Kitson? Three contestants put their lives in the balance. Now, who among them is strong enough to keep their blades from falling? Blades made from Veroovian steel... The densest metal in the galaxy. The player that cannot hold the weight, will decapitate. The bets are in! Let the fun begin!
Who will be the first to die? The scoundrel who tried to steal my most coveted brothel worker?
Guilty as charged.
Or will it be one of the Terrans? Criminals wanted for nefarious intergalactic deeds.
If I get out of this, I could use the company of a pretty lady.
Hey! Hey! Hey! Knock it off!
Fitz, don't!
Fitz, dooo.
I want the Terrans.
What? Who are you?
Your man makes a move, this blade goes in straight, comes out sideways.
The wagers have already been made. You can't just stop the game.
I'm not asking you to stop the game. Just pick the loser. Everyone knows that the games of Kitson are rigged.
That's an outrageous assertion, my grandfather helped build...
I know you've got that little button clutched in your hand.
Okay, fair enough. But you can't just expect me to give them to you.
I didn't ask that. I'll buy them.
Why?
That's my business. I understand yours is to make a profit. I'll compensate you fairly.
Okay. Okay.
Damn.
Compliments on the choice.
The Terrans have prevailed in an upset. Another example that in my House of Games, anyone can be lucky.
You look like you could use a drink.
